# ANALYSIS: J_DAYS_ENGINE_PERFORMANCE_UNKNOWN

**Filed:** 2026-05-31  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** quality_gate  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

The current engine's P&L on J's source-of-truth anchor days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04 winners; 5/05, 5/06, 5/07 losers) is unknown without an expanding window walk-forward simulation using realistic OPRA real-fills. This baseline measurement is essential to evaluate any strategy changes against OP-16 edge_capture requirements and avoid proposing changes that degrade performance on these critical days.

## Mechanism

Run expanding window walk-forward simulation:
- In-sample window: fixed initial period (e.g., 6 months), expanding by 1 month each iteration
- Out-of-sample: next month after in-sample window
- For each OOS window, simulate engine decisions on J days falling within that window
- Use realistic OPRA fills simulator (cached real bid-ask/ask, slippage model, latency) instead of BS-sim
- Compute engine P&L per J day: sum of P&L from engine-triggered trades
- Anchor P&L: +$342 (4/29), +$470 (5/01), +$730 (5/04) for winners; $0 for losers (engine must skip or lose less)
- Delta = engine P&L - anchor P&L (positive delta = improvement over anchor, negative = degradation)

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** Simulation assumes qty=28 requires $25K+ account; $1K paper account scales to ~14% of headline P&L (per OP-20 disclosure 1 and LESSONS-LEARNED L11).
2. **Sample bias:** Expanding window walk-forward uses temporal splitting to reduce overfit risk; initial IS window = 6 months, OOS windows expand sequentially. Overfit risk mitigated by walk-forward stability checks (OOS/IS Sharpe ratio > 0.5 gate).
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (simulation not yet performed; will report OOS/IS Sharpe ratio and P&L stability upon completion).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (realistic OPRA fills simulation not yet performed; will use cached OPRA data with bid-ask/ask and slippage model).
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day = max single-day loss on J losers; max drawdown = peak-to-trough during simulation; blow-up scenario = engine taking large losers (> -$100/day) on multiple J loser days.
6. **Concentration:** Not applicable for this analysis (baseline measurement); will be reported for any subsequent candidate proposals.

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Gym validators: 67/67 PASS (current engine)
- Walk-forward: OOS/IS Sharpe ratio ≥ 0.50 on J days
- Real-fills: Simulated OPRA fills vs BS-sim diff < ±20% on top 3 J days (4/29, 5/01, 5/04)
- OP-16 edge_capture ≥ 771 (50% of max 1542) for any candidate derived from this baseline

## Confidence

3 / 10 -- No simulation results available; confidence will increase after Stage-1 backtest completes and OP-20 disclosures are satisfied.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This analysis does not conflict with existing candidates; it provides a baseline for evaluating all J-impacting candidates (e.g., BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON, ORB gates, MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE). Candidates like #12 V14E_PARAM_SWEEP_26K and #21 MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE already report partial J-day impacts; this analysis will fill gaps for missing days (5/01, 5/05, 5/06, 5/07 losers). No duplication of effort; complements leaderboard by standardizing measurement methodology.
